instruction:
Use a scatterplot and the linear correlation coefficient $r$ to determine whether there is a correlation between the two variables. (Note: Use software, and don't forget to look at the scatterplot!)
\begin{array}{c|ccccccccccccccc} x & 0.5 & 1.6 & 2.2 & 3.5 & 4.4 & 5.2 & 6.4 & 7.4 & 8.1 & 9.3 & 10.7 & 11.3 & 12.8 & 13.2 & 14.2\cr
y & 9.3 & 4.5 & 8.5 & 5.6 & 9.8 & 7.6 & 5.9 & 7.7 & 2.3 & 7.7 & 6.9 & 7 & 15 & 0.4 & 8.2\cr \end{array}
(a) $r=$ [ANS]
(b) There is [ANS] A. no correlation between $x$ and $y$  B. a perfect positive correlation between $x$ and $y$  C. a positive correlation between $x$ and $y$  D. a negative correlation between $x$ and $y$  E. a perfect negative correlation between $x$ and $y$  F. a nonlinear correlation between $x$ and $y$
